verses.1: “However, please hear my speech, Job, And listen to all my words.
verses.2: “Behold now, I open my mouth, My tongue in my mouth speaks.
verses.3: “My words are from the integrity of my heart, And my lips speak knowledge sincerely.
verses.4: “The Spirit of God has made me, And the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
verses.5: “Refute me if you can; Line up against me, take your stand.
verses.6: “Behold, I belong to God, like you; I too have been formed out of the clay.
verses.7: “Behold, no fear of me should terrify you, Nor should my pressure weigh heavily on you.
verses.8: “You have in fact spoken while I listened, And I heard the sound of your words:
verses.9: ‘I am pure, without wrongdoing; I am innocent and there is no guilt in me.
verses.10: ‘Behold, He invents criticisms against me; He counts me as His enemy.
verses.11: ‘He puts my feet in the stocks; He watches all my paths.’
verses.12: “Behold, let me respond to you, you are not right in this, For God is greater than mankind.
verses.13: “Why do you complain to Him That He does not give an account of all His doings?
verses.14: “Indeed God speaks once, Or twice, yet no one notices it.
verses.15: “In a dream, a vision of the night, When deep sleep falls on people, While they slumber in their beds,
verses.16: Then He opens the ears of people, And horrifies them with warnings,
verses.17: So that He may turn a person away from bad conduct, And keep a man from pride;
verses.18: He keeps his soul back from the pit, And his life from perishing by the spear.
verses.19: “ A person is also rebuked by pain in his bed, And with constant complaint in his bones,
verses.20: So that his life loathes bread, And his soul, food that he should crave.
verses.21: “His flesh wastes away from sight, And his bones, which were not seen, stick out.
verses.22: “Then his soul comes near to the pit, And his life to those who bring death.
verses.23: “If there is an interceding angel for him, One out of a thousand, To remind a person of what is right for him,
verses.24: And he is gracious to him, and says, ‘Free him from going down to the pit, I have found a ransom’;
verses.25: Let his flesh become fresher than in youth, Let him return to the days of his youthful vigor;
verses.26: Then he will pray to God, and He will accept him, So that he may see His face with joy, And He will restore His righteousness to that person.
verses.27: “He will sing to people and say, ‘I have sinned and perverted what is right, And it is not proper for me.
verses.28: ‘He has redeemed my soul from going to the pit, And my life will see the light.’
verses.29: “Behold, God does all these things for a man two or three times,
verses.30: To bring back his soul from the pit, So that he may be enlightened with the light of life.
verses.31: “Pay attention, Job, listen to me; Keep silent, and let me speak.
verses.32: “ Then if you have anything to say, answer me; Speak, for I would take pleasure in justifying you.
verses.33: “If not, listen to me; Keep silent, and I will teach you wisdom.”
